name: Great Hammerhead Shark
summary: The great hammerhead shark is a large, powerful predator famous for its unique hammer-shaped head, called a cephalofoil. Inhabiting warm tropical oceans...
metaTitle: Great Hammerhead Shark Facts | Habitat, Diet, Head & Photos
metaDescription: Great hammerhead shark facts for kids with photos, tropical ocean habitat, diet, hammer-shaped head, sensory powers, and quick facts on one easy-to-read page.
searchIntents: what do great hammerhead sharks eat
where do great hammerhead sharks live
speed: Up to 25 mph (40 km/h) swimming speed
behaviors: hunting stingrays on the sea floor
migrating to cooler waters in summer
adaptations: extra sensory pores (Ampullae of Lorenzini) to detect prey
tall curved dorsal fin for stable swimming
sharp serrated teeth for catching prey
camouflage grey back and white belly (countershading)
funFacts: A hammerhead's hammer-shaped head is called a cephalofoil! It places the shark's eyes and nostrils far apart, giving them a 360-degree view of their surroundings.
Great hammerheads are immune to the venomous stings of stingrays! They often hunt them by using their wide heads to pin stingrays flat against the sandy ocean floor.
They have extra electrical sensors (Ampullae of Lorenzini) spread out across their wide heads, allowing them to detect the tiny heartbeats of prey buried in the sand.
Unlike most sharks, which are born from eggs, baby hammerheads are born alive! They are called pups and are fully independent from the moment they are born.
Hammerhead sharks have a massive, curved dorsal fin on their backs that is taller than that of most other sharks, helping them make sharp, quick turns.
Because they have to swim constantly to breathe, hammerhead sharks never truly sleep; they just rest parts of their brains while swimming slowly.

faq.question: What is a great hammerhead shark?
faq.answer: The great hammerhead shark is the largest of the hammerhead shark species, famous for its large hammer-shaped head and predatory skills.

faq.question: Where do great hammerhead sharks live?
faq.answer: They live in warm, tropical, and warm-temperate ocean waters worldwide, both near the coast and in the open ocean near coral reefs.

faq.question: What do great hammerhead sharks eat?
faq.answer: They are carnivores that eat a variety of marine animals, but their absolute favorite food is stingrays, along with fish, squid, and smaller sharks.
